stated_limitations: Uncertainty-aware reward models have been adopted without thorough comparison, leading to poor understanding of their effectiveness
unstated_weaknesses: Focuses on reward models for LLMs rather than image classification
Application domain is different (language models vs. medical images)
Unclear transferability of methods to medical imaging domain
threats_to_validity: Generalization from language models to medical image classification might not be straightforward
Paper doesn't address domain-specific challenges in medical imaging
open_questions: How well do these uncertainty quantification methods transfer to medical image classification?
How can calibration-aware active learning be specifically tailored for medical images?
future_work: Further investigation into model size and initialization impacts on uncertainty quantification
gaps_this_enables: Framework for evaluating uncertainty quantification in medical image classification
Informing the design of calibration-aware active learning strategies for medical images
relevance_to_topic: Provides a unified framework for uncertainty quantification that could be adapted for calibration-aware active learning in medical image classification
limitations_stated: Uncertainty-aware reward models have been adopted without thorough comparison, leading to poor understanding of their effectiveness
title: RewardUQ: A Unified Framework for Uncertainty-Aware Reward Models
